desc: Enforce strict production query-specific CLI argument validation consistently across `symbols`, `references`, `implementations`, and `packages` so malformed query syntax cannot be silently accepted as successful JSON.
done_when: Production command-level tests prove documented invocations still succeed: `symbols --index <fixture> --name <value>`, `references --index <fixture> --symbol <full-scip-symbol>`, `implementations --index <fixture> --symbol <full-scip-symbol>`, `packages --index <fixture>`, and `packages --index <fixture> --prefix <value>`. The same tests prove invalid query-specific argument shapes fail as usage failures with status 2, empty stdout, and nonempty stderr before successful JSON is written: `symbols` accepts exactly `--name <non-empty non-flag value>`; `references` and `implementations` each accept exactly `--symbol <non-empty non-flag value>`; `packages` accepts either no query args or exactly `--prefix <non-empty non-flag value>`; duplicate value flags, missing values, empty string values, flag-shaped values such as `--name --unknown`, `--symbol --unknown`, and `--prefix --name`, unknown trailing flags after valid args, and stray positional args before or after valid args are rejected for every applicable command. Valid full SCIP symbols, including symbols containing spaces, are preserved exactly; shared `--index` parsing, traversal/query result selection, JSON payload schemas, loader behavior, installer/distribution behavior, and accepting any new query flags remain unchanged; `make test validate-distribution` passes.
scope: cmd/scip-search query-specific argument parsing helpers and production command-level tests for `symbols`, `references`, `implementations`, and `packages`. Out of scope: shared `--index` parsing, traversal/query result selection, JSON payload schemas, loader behavior, installer/distribution behavior, partial/fuzzy/regex matching, and accepting any new query flags.
